question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here are all the hook events a Juju charm can observe:

## Lifecycle Hooks
- **install** — runs once when the unit is first deployed
- **config-changed** — runs when configuration changes (and after install, upgrade, and relation changes)
- **start** — runs after install and config-changed on first deployment
- **stop** — runs when the unit is about to be destroyed
- **remove** — runs when the unit is being removed
- **upgrade-charm** — runs when the charm code is upgraded

## Relation Hooks
For each relation (replace `{relation}` with the relation name):
- **{relation}-relation-created** — a new relation has been created
- **{relation}-relation-joined** — a new remote unit has joined the relation
- **{relation}-relation-changed** — relation data has changed
- **{relation}-relation-departed** — a remote unit is leaving the relation
- **{relation}-relation-broken** — the relation is being removed entirely

## Storage Hooks
For each storage (replace `{storage}` with the storage name):
- **{storage}-storage-attached** — storage has been attached
- **{storage}-storage-detaching** — storage is about to be detached

## Container/Pebble Hooks (Kubernetes charms only)
For each container (replace `{container}` with the container name):
- **{container}-pebble-ready** — the Pebble API in the container is available
- **{container}-pebble-custom-notice** — a custom Pebble notice was recorded

## Action Hooks
For each action (replace `{action}` with the action name):
- **{action}-action** — an action has been invoked

## Secret Hooks
- **secret-changed** — a secret the charm is tracking has a new revision
- **secret-expired** — a secret owned by the charm has expired
- **secret-rotate** — a secret owned by the charm needs rotation
- **secret-remove** — a secret revision owned by the charm can be removed

## Leader Hooks
- **leader-elected** — this unit has become the leader
- **leader-settings-changed** — leader settings have changed (legacy, rarely used)

## Other Hooks
- **update-status** — periodic hook (default every 5 minutes) for health checks
- **collect-unit-status** — called before reporting unit status (Ops manages this)
- **collect-app-status** — called before reporting app status (leader only, Ops manages this)
- **pre-series-upgrade** / **post-series-upgrade** — for OS series upgrades (machine charms, legacy)

In Ops, these are observed as Python events on `self.on`, e.g. `self.on.install`, `self.on.config_changed`, `self.on["{relation}"].relation_changed`, `self.on["{container}"].pebble_ready`, etc.
